source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Mission at Rwazi


Rwazi is a product-driven AI data intelligence company operating at the intersection of zero-party consumer data, enterprise SaaS, and decision systems.

The Head of Marketing owns the complete marketing function — positioning, product marketing, demand generation, brand authority, revenue enablement, and market intelligence.

Because Rwazi is product-led, this role ensures product capabilities translate into revenue clarity, market differentiation, and scalable pipeline growth.

The Head of Marketing builds and governs the system that turns product intelligence into market dominance.

The Director of Product Marketing reports into this role.




YOU WILL BE IN CHARGE OF

 - Defining Rwazi’s market narrative and category positioning
   

 - Owning the end-to-end go-to-market system
   

 - Driving measurable pipeline contribution
   

 - Building a repeatable product launch engine
   

 - Governing sales enablement and revenue alignment
   

 - Establishing Rwazi as a trusted authority in AI-driven consumer intelligence
   

This is a system-building leadership role, not a campaign management function.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES




MARKETING SYSTEM OWNERSHIP

 - Architect the full marketing operating system (strategy → backlog → execution → metrics)
   

 - Ensure marketing outputs are structured, scalable, and measurable
   

 - Build a disciplined marketing planning cadence aligned to revenue goals
   


PRODUCT MARKETING LEADERSHIP

 - Oversee the Director of Product Marketing
   

 - Ensure product launches are structured, repeatable, and revenue-aligned
   

 - Maintain messaging clarity across feature releases and platform evolution
   

 - Translate technical capabilities into buyer-centric narratives
   


POSITIONING & CATEGORY STRATEGY

 - Define and refine Rwazi’s positioning within AI data intelligence
   

 - Establish defensible differentiation against competitors
   

 - Shape category narratives that elevate Rwazi beyond feature-level comparison
   


DEMAND GENERATION & PIPELINE IMPACT

 - Design pipeline generation strategies aligned with enterprise sales cycles
   

 - Define marketing-sourced and marketing-influenced revenue targets
   

 - Optimize channel mix (content, outbound enablement, partnerships, thought leadership)
   


SALES ENABLEMENT & REVENUE ALIGNMENT

 - Ensure Sales and Customer Success are consistently trained and equipped
   

 - Align messaging, collateral, and objection handling frameworks
   

 - Push back on reactive requests that do not align with structured priorities
   


MARKET INTELLIGENCE & FEEDBACK LOOPS

 - Own win/loss analysis, competitive intelligence, and customer insights
   

 - Feed structured intelligence back into Product and Executive strategy
   

 - Identify expansion and positioning opportunities
   


BRAND AUTHORITY & THOUGHT LEADERSHIP

 - Establish Rwazi as a category authority through structured content programs
   

 - Govern enterprise-grade collateral, case studies, and narrative consistency
   

 - Ensure all public-facing outputs reinforce product credibility
   




ROLE IMPACT



Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Clear and defensible market positioning
   

 - Structured, repeatable product launches
   

 - Measurable pipeline contribution
   

 - Higher win rates through messaging precision
   

 - Stronger executive alignment between Product, Sales, and Marketing
   

This role determines whether Rwazi is perceived as a feature vendor or a category-defining AI platform.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT



 - This is not a social media management role
   

 - This is not a purely creative brand function
   

 - This is not disconnected from revenue accountability
   

 - This is not reactive campaign execution
   

This role requires strategic discipline, structured execution, and commercial impact.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE



We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - 8–12+ years in B2B SaaS marketing, ideall

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Design (SaaS)
source_jobs.description:
Director of Product Design (SaaS)
Location: Global (Remote)
Reports to: CEO
Employment Type: Full-time






MISSION AT RWAZI

Rwazi’s AI-powered Customer SaaS Platform equips enterprise brands with decision-grade, zero-party consumer intelligence.

The Director of Product Design owns the design system, user psychology architecture, and product experience across the enterprise platform.

This is a senior individual contributor role. It is not a people-management layer.

The Director of Product Design is expected to be deeply hands-on — designing core workflows, dashboards, data visualizations, and habit-forming product mechanics that drive enterprise adoption, stickiness, and expansion.

This role defines how Rwazi feels, flows, and grows inside enterprise accounts.






CORE MANDATE

The Director of Product Design is accountable for:

 - Designing an enterprise-grade SaaS experience that drives repeat usage

 - Embedding viral and expansion mechanics within a B2B platform

 - Aligning UX with revenue metrics (account expansion, frequency, referrals)

 - Building and maintaining a scalable design system

 - Elevating design as a strategic growth lever

This role directly impacts product adoption and account value growth.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


ENTERPRISE USER PSYCHOLOGY & VIRALITY ARCHITECTURE

 - Design habit-forming workflows that increase session frequency

 - Embed internal sharing, collaboration, and referral triggers

 - Align feature UX with enterprise buying cycles and expansion logic

 - Convert satisfied users into internal advocates across departments


DESIGN SYSTEM OWNERSHIP

 - Define and maintain scalable UX and visual systems

 - Establish global UX patterns suitable for enterprise audiences

 - Ensure consistency across dashboards, analytics, and workflow modules

 - Continuously refine usability as platform complexity grows


PRODUCT GROWTH ALIGNMENT

 - Design interfaces that encourage deeper data exploration

 - Optimize dashboards and alerts to increase recurring engagement

 - Align UX decisions with measurable product metrics

 - Partner with Product and Sales to align design with revenue goals


IMPLEMENTATION & ITERATION

 - Own the full design lifecycle from concept to post-launch optimization

 - Prototype rapidly and test with real users

 - Partner closely with Engineering to ensure high-fidelity execution

 - Use analytics, A/B testing, and feedback loops to refine continuously


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL INFLUENCE

 - Collaborate with Product, Engineering, Sales, and Customer Success

 - Present design strategy and outcomes to executive leadership

 - Translate complex data functionality into intuitive experiences




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Increased usage frequency per enterprise account

 - Higher multi-department adoption

 - Improved retention and expansion rates

 - Clear, intuitive enterprise dashboards

 - Stronger product-led growth signals

This role determines whether Rwazi becomes a tool or a habit.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not a surface-level UI design role

 - This is not brand or marketing design

 - This is not passive design support

 - This is not detached from commercial outcomes

This role requires strategic product thinking and execution depth.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - 5+ years in SaaS product design, with enterprise exposure

 - Demonstrated ability to design sticky, referral-driven B2B products

 - Strong experience designing dashboards and data visualizations

 - Deep understanding of enterprise adoption psychology

 - Exceptional proficiency in Figma and scalable design systems

 - Analytical fluency with A/B testing and usage metrics

 - Ability to operate as a high-output individual contributor

Candidates may come from high-growth B2B SaaS platforms where product experience directly influenced retention and expansion.




CULTURAL FIT

We value designers who:

 - Th

source_jobs.title: Head of Research & Development (R&D)
source_jobs.description:
Head of Research & Development (R&D)
Location: Global (Remote)
Reports to: CEO
Employment Type: Full-time






MISSION AT RWAZI

Rwazi’s long-term advantage depends on expanding the frontier of what decision systems can reason about.

The Head of R&D owns long-horizon capability development — exploring, prototyping, and formalizing the next generation of decision intelligence that will power future versions of Rwazi’s platform.

This role operates beyond immediate roadmap pressures.

It identifies structural opportunities in reasoning systems, signal modeling, AI-assisted judgment, and decision automation — and transforms them into foundational capabilities.

The Head of R&D defines what Rwazi becomes 3–5 years from now.




CORE MANDATE

The Head of R&D is accountable for:

 - Advancing future decision system architectures

 - Formalizing higher-order reasoning capabilities

 - Exploring new signal classes and evaluation logic

 - Identifying structural AI leverage points

 - Converting research into reusable system primitives

This is a capability-building role, not a feature-delivery function.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


LONG-HORIZON CAPABILITY EXPLORATION

 - Identify unsolved reasoning challenges within Rwazi’s domain

 - Explore advanced decision frameworks beyond current product constraints

 - Develop conceptual models for higher-order AI reasoning systems

 - Investigate new signal environments and structured judgment methods


PROTOTYPE & FORMALIZATION

 - Build experimental prototypes to test new system architectures

 - Formalize reasoning patterns into structured logic frameworks

 - Create reusable primitives that Product and Engineering can adopt

 - Document system logic in technically rigorous formats


AI & DECISION SYSTEMS ADVANCEMENT

 - Push beyond current signal-to-output pipelines

 - Explore multi-layer reasoning and tradeoff modeling

 - Design evaluation criteria for increasingly complex decisions

 - Anticipate future enterprise use cases and model their structural requirements


STRATEGIC INTERFACE WITH EXECUTIVE LEADERSHIP

 - Advise CEO on long-term technical differentiation strategy

 - Translate research insights into strategic investment decisions

 - Identify potential defensibility moats


RESEARCH GOVERNANCE

 - Define standards for experimentation rigor

 - Establish evaluation frameworks for prototype validation

 - Maintain clarity between exploratory work and production systems




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Structural differentiation in decision intelligence

 - New classes of solvable enterprise questions

 - Expansion of AI-assisted reasoning depth

 - Long-term defensibility against competitors

 - Research-driven product evolution

This role ensures Rwazi does not stagnate at feature-level innovation.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not roadmap feature delivery

 - This is not short-term optimization

 - This is not pure academic research detached from application

 - This is not reactive problem-solving

This role requires frontier thinking and disciplined formalization.


QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Deep systems thinking and formal reasoning ability

 - Experience designing structured decision frameworks

 - Strong understanding of AI systems, modeling logic, or computational reasoning

 - Ability to translate abstract research into usable system components

 - Comfort operating autonomously at high strategic altitude

 - Demonstrated work on complex, ambiguous problems

Candidates may come from applied research, AI system design, computational modeling, advanced analytics, or technically rigorous environments.




CULTURAL FIT

We value leaders who:

 - Think in decades, not quarters

 - Formalize ideas with intellectual rigor

 - Challenge existing system boundaries

 - Operate independently without constant direction

 - Care about structural innovation over incremental gain

This role i

source_jobs.title: Product Analyst
source_jobs.description:
Product Analyst
Location: Global (Remote)
Reports to: VP of Product
Employment Type: Full-time






MISSION AT RWAZI

Rwazi’s AI-powered SaaS platform serves enterprise clients who rely on decision-grade data and actionable intelligence.

The Product Analyst owns the quantitative visibility layer of the product.

This role analyzes user behavior, product performance metrics, feature adoption, and retention signals to inform product strategy and roadmap decisions.

The Product Analyst ensures product decisions are grounded in evidence, not intuition.




CORE MANDATE

The Product Analyst is accountable for:

 - Measuring and interpreting user behavior across the SaaS platform

 - Tracking feature adoption, engagement, and retention metrics

 - Identifying friction points and drop-offs

 - Translating behavioral data into actionable product insights

 - Providing structured recommendations to VP of Product

This is a product intelligence role embedded inside product execution.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


USER BEHAVIOR ANALYSIS

 - Track user journeys across dashboards, workflows, and modules

 - Identify usage frequency patterns across accounts

 - Analyze session depth, repeat usage, and expansion signals

 - Detect behavioral differences across verticals and enterprise segments


FEATURE PERFORMANCE & ADOPTION

 - Monitor new feature launch performance

 - Track adoption velocity and engagement rates

 - Identify underutilized features and diagnose causes

 - Support A/B testing and experiment analysis


RETENTION & EXPANSION METRICS

 - Measure account-level retention and churn indicators

 - Identify signals predictive of expansion or contraction

 - Build cohort analyses across industries and client tiers


PRODUCT KPI GOVERNANCE

 - Define and maintain core product metrics

 - Ensure data accuracy and consistency across dashboards

 - Maintain product performance reporting cadence (weekly/monthly)


INSIGHT GENERATION & STRATEGIC INPUT

 - Translate data into clear, structured insights

 - Provide recommendations to VP of Product and cross-functional teams

 - Surface early warning signals affecting product health




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Data-driven roadmap decisions

 - Faster detection of product friction

 - Improved feature adoption rates

 - Higher retention and expansion metrics

 - Reduced reliance on anecdotal product feedback

This role strengthens the intelligence behind product evolution.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not a passive reporting role

 - This is not general business analytics disconnected from product

 - This is not ad-hoc dashboard building only

This role requires structured reasoning, proactive insight generation, and product fluency.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - 3–6+ years in product analytics or SaaS analytics roles

 - Strong fluency in SQL, data modeling, and analytics tooling

 - Experience working closely with Product teams

 - Ability to conduct cohort analysis, funnel analysis, and retention modeling

 - Strong quantitative reasoning and pattern recognition

 - Comfort translating data into executive-level insights

Candidates may come from high-growth B2B SaaS, marketplace platforms, or data-intensive environments.




CULTURAL FIT

We value analysts who:

 - Think in systems and causal relationships

 - Question assumptions and validate with data

 - Prefer clarity over complexity

 - Move quickly and iterate

 - Are comfortable operating with executive visibility

This role is suited for individuals who enjoy turning behavioral data into product advantage.




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to interpret behavioral product data

 - Clarity and rigor of their analytical reasoning

 - Experience building actionable product insights

 - Comfort working with ambiguity and incomplete signals

 - Their ability to influence product decisio

source_jobs.title: Decision Systems Architect
source_jobs.description:
DECISION SYSTEMS ARCHITECT

Team: Product
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: Product Leadership




ROLE OVERVIEW

Sena is designed to translate complex business intent into decision-grade outputs at scale.

The Decision Systems Architect plays a critical role in advancing Sena’s decision capabilities by formalizing judgment, reasoning frameworks, and evaluation logic into system-native structures. This role focuses on extending the breadth, depth, and consistency of Sena’s decision logic as new customer use cases, signal types, and business questions emerge.

This is a product-embedded role at the intersection of systems thinking, applied reasoning, and AI enablement.






KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The Decision Systems Architect is responsible for:

 - Designing and refining decision frameworks that guide how Sena interprets customer intent
   

 - Defining signal mapping logic that structures how business questions are decomposed into actionable signals
   

 - Establishing configuration schemas that specify how data must be captured to support decision-grade outputs
   

 - Creating evaluation criteria and quality standards used to assess system outputs
   

 - Identifying recurring patterns, edge cases, and failure modes, and formalizing them into reusable system logic
   

 - Producing structured artifacts that directly inform Product and AI development workflows
   

 - Collaborating closely with Product, AI, and Engineering teams to ensure decision logic is encoded effectively into Sena
   

All work in this role is expected to be system-oriented, reusable, and extensible.






ROLE IMPACT AND GROWTH

This role contributes to Sena’s evolution as new business problems, customer objectives, and data environments emerge.

As the system’s capabilities expand, the focus of this role naturally shifts toward:

 - New classes of customer questions
   

 - New signal types and constraints
   

 - More complex decision structures and tradeoffs
   

 - Higher-order reasoning patterns that have not yet been formalized
   

This role remains at the frontier of decision design as the product evolves.








QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Strong systems thinking and the ability to formalize complex reasoning
   

 - Experience designing frameworks, methodologies, or structured decision logic
   

 - The ability to translate ambiguous problems into clear, explicit structures
   

 - Comfort working in highly autonomous, fast-moving environments
   

 - Intellectual curiosity and a willingness to challenge established methods
   

Candidates may come from applied research or technically rigorous domains where deep reasoning, formal abstraction, and the hands-on design of complex decision systems were core to the work.






CULTURAL FIT

We value people who:

 - Take initiative and operate with high ownership
   

 - Produce high output with limited resources
   

 - Prefer autonomy and accountability
   

 - Are inventive and comfortable rejecting outdated approaches
   

 - Are deeply focused and driven by hard problems
   

This role is best suited for individuals who enjoy building foundational systems and pushing the boundaries of what software can reason about.






HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to structure and reason through ambiguous problems
   

 - The clarity, generalizability, and technical rigor of the frameworks they design
   

 - How effectively their thinking can be translated into system-level logic
   

 - The quality and rigor of their judgment under real-world constraints
   

 - Their ability to independently design, execute, and iterate on decision frameworks
   

We prioritize demonstrated capability and depth of thinking over resumes alone.




SUMMARY

The Decision Systems Architect helps define how Sena thinks as it continues to expand across new domains and use cases. This role is central to shaping t

source_jobs.title: Decision Research Scientist
source_jobs.description:
DECISION RESEARCH SCIENTIST

Team: Research & Development
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: Head of R&D




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi advances decision systems — software capable of structured reasoning under real-world constraints.

The Decision Research Scientist applies formal reasoning, modeling, and experimentation to real enterprise decision problems.

This role operates at the boundary between research and application.

It translates abstract decision theory, evaluation logic, and structured reasoning into practical system improvements that enhance Rwazi’s decision intelligence.

This is applied decision research — not academic isolation.




CORE MANDATE

The Decision Research Scientist is accountable for:

 - Designing formal decision frameworks for complex enterprise problems

 - Modeling tradeoffs, uncertainty, and signal ambiguity

 - Advancing structured reasoning methodologies

 - Testing and validating new decision architectures

 - Converting research insight into system-ready primitives

This role strengthens the reasoning depth of Rwazi’s decision engine.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


APPLIED DECISION MODELING

 - Formalize complex business questions into structured decision systems

 - Model uncertainty, tradeoffs, and multi-variable constraints

 - Design evaluation logic for ambiguous signal environments

 - Develop structured judgment methodologies


RESEARCH-TO-APPLICATION TRANSLATION

 - Apply theoretical frameworks to real client use cases

 - Test decision logic against live or simulated environments

 - Identify structural weaknesses in existing decision pathways

 - Propose formal improvements with measurable rigor


EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN

 - Design controlled experiments to validate reasoning enhancements

 - Define metrics for decision quality and output consistency

 - Compare alternative system architectures under defined constraints


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL INTEGRATION

 - Collaborate with Research Engineers to prototype research concepts

 - Provide formal specifications to Product and Engineering

 - Advise leadership on long-term decision capability evolution




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - More reliable and explainable decision outputs

 - Increased reasoning depth across signal types

 - Expansion into new classes of enterprise problems

 - Stronger structural defensibility

This role deepens Rwazi’s intellectual core.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not feature delivery

 - This is not surface-level analytics

 - This is not academic research detached from application

This role requires rigorous thinking applied to real-world constraints.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Strong grounding in decision theory, systems modeling, or applied reasoning

 - Experience formalizing ambiguous problems into structured frameworks

 - Comfort working with AI systems and reasoning architectures

 - Ability to design and evaluate experiments rigorously

 - Intellectual independence and systems thinking

Candidates may come from applied AI research, quantitative modeling, computational social science, economics, operations research, or advanced analytics domains.




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to formalize complex decision problems

 - The rigor and clarity of their reasoning models

 - Evidence of applying theory to practical systems

 - Depth of structured thinking

 - Their ability to improve decision quality measurably




SUMMARY

The Decision Research Scientist advances Rwazi’s applied decision intelligence by turning complex reasoning into structured, validated system logic.

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
RESEARCH ENGINEER

Team: Research & Development
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: Head of R&D




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi’s research initiatives require rapid experimentation, prototyping, and system validation.

The Research Engineer enables applied research through technical system construction.

This role builds prototypes, experimental pipelines, evaluation tooling, and research infrastructure that allow decision concepts to be tested under real-world constraints.

The Research Engineer operationalizes research velocity.




CORE MANDATE

The Research Engineer is accountable for:

 - Building research prototypes and sandbox environments

 - Developing evaluation tooling for decision systems

 - Implementing experimental system architectures

 - Enabling fast iteration between theory and validation

 - Maintaining technical research infrastructure

This role bridges conceptual research and system reality.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PROTOTYPE DEVELOPMENT

 - Implement experimental decision pipelines

 - Build modular, testable research architectures

 - Translate conceptual models into runnable systems


RESEARCH TOOLING & INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Create evaluation frameworks for reasoning outputs

 - Build simulation environments for controlled testing

 - Develop benchmarking systems for decision performance


EXPERIMENT ENABLEMENT

 - Implement A/B logic and structured experiment tracking

 - Optimize iteration cycles for research testing

 - Ensure reproducibility of experimental results


SYSTEM INTEGRATION SUPPORT

 - Collaborate with Decision Research Scientists to encode models

 - Provide technical validation of research concepts

 - Identify engineering constraints early


ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Faster research iteration cycles

 - More reliable prototype validation

 - Reduced gap between research and production

 - Higher experimental rigor

This role accelerates Rwazi’s long-term capability development.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not product feature engineering

 - This is not maintenance development

 - This is not short-term optimization

This role builds experimental systems.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals

 - Experience building experimental or prototype systems

 - Comfort working in ambiguous research environments

 - Ability to balance speed and technical rigor

 - Familiarity with AI pipelines or decision architectures

Candidates may come from research labs, AI startups, advanced analytics teams, or system architecture roles.




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to rapidly implement experimental systems

 - Technical clarity and modularity of their code

 - Comfort working with incomplete specifications

 - Understanding of evaluation logic and system validation

 - Ability to collaborate with research-oriented thinkers




SUMMARY

The Research Engineer enables Rwazi’s applied decision research by building the technical infrastructure required to explore, validate, and evolve next-generation decision systems.

If you'd like, we can next define:

 - Decision Systems Fellow (elite individual contributor track)

 - AI Evaluation & Alignment Lead

 - Advanced Simulation Architect

 - Future Platforms Lab

source_jobs.title: Decision Intelligence Analyst
source_jobs.description:
DECISION INTELLIGENCE ANALYST

Team: Product
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: VP of Product




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi’s platform produces decision-grade outputs powered by structured reasoning and AI-assisted judgment.

The Decision Intelligence Analyst owns decision quality.

This role evaluates system outputs, identifies reasoning weaknesses, and strengthens AI judgment through structured feedback and training loops.

It ensures the platform does not merely generate outputs — but generates sound, defensible decisions.

The Decision Intelligence Analyst is the quality control layer for decision intelligence.




CORE MANDATE

The Decision Intelligence Analyst is accountable for:

 - Evaluating decision outputs for logical integrity and sound reasoning

 - Identifying patterns of judgment failure or inconsistency

 - Designing structured feedback loops to improve AI reasoning

 - Training and refining AI judgment frameworks

 - Defining measurable standards for decision quality

This role governs the reliability of Rwazi’s decision engine.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


DECISION OUTPUT EVALUATION

 - Review system outputs for logical coherence and reasoning rigor

 - Assess signal interpretation accuracy

 - Identify tradeoff miscalculations or flawed inference pathways

 - Document recurring reasoning gaps


AI JUDGMENT TRAINING

 - Create structured examples to refine reasoning performance

 - Develop edge-case libraries for training robustness

 - Formalize evaluation rubrics for decision quality

 - Collaborate with R&D to improve reasoning architecture


QUALITY STANDARDS & METRICS

 - Define measurable criteria for decision-grade output

 - Track improvements in reasoning consistency

 - Monitor drift in output quality over time

 - Establish acceptance thresholds for release


FAILURE MODE ANALYSIS

 - Identify systemic reasoning weaknesses

 - Surface blind spots in signal modeling

 - Propose structured adjustments to logic layers

 - Escalate structural flaws early


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLLABORATION

 - Partner with Product to align quality with roadmap goals

 - Collaborate with R&D on advanced reasoning improvements

 - Provide structured feedback to Engineering when system behavior deviates




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Higher confidence in decision outputs

 - Reduced reasoning inconsistencies

 - Improved explainability

 - Faster detection of system drift

 - Stronger enterprise trust

This role protects the intellectual credibility of the platform.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not general QA

 - This is not surface-level data validation

 - This is not simple output review

This role evaluates reasoning quality, not formatting correctness.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Strong analytical and logical reasoning ability

 - Experience evaluating AI systems, decision frameworks, or complex models

 - Comfort dissecting multi-step reasoning chains

 - Ability to formalize judgment criteria

 - Strong written clarity and structured thinking

 - Comfort working with ambiguity and edge cases

Candidates may come from applied AI evaluation, consulting, operations research, economics, philosophy of logic, or technically rigorous analytical fields.




CULTURAL FIT

We value analysts who:

 - Obsess over reasoning integrity

 - Question outputs rather than accept them

 - Care about intellectual rigor

 - Prefer structured evaluation over intuition

 - Are comfortable holding high standards




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to critique and improve structured reasoning

 - Clarity of their evaluation frameworks

 - Depth of logical analysis

 - Ability to identify hidden failure modes

 - Their rigor in defining measurable quality standards

We prioritize demonstrated reasoning discipline over titles alone.




SUMMARY

The Decision Intelligence Analyst safeguards the qual

source_jobs.title: Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
RECRUITER

Team: People
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: Recruitment Lead




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi scales through disciplined talent acquisition.

The Recruiter executes the hiring engine — sourcing, screening, coordinating, and progressing high-quality candidates through structured evaluation pipelines.

This role ensures hiring velocity without compromising talent bar.

The Recruiter does not simply fill roles.
They move qualified candidates through a structured, high-signal system.




CORE MANDATE

The Recruiter is accountable for:

 - Generating qualified candidate flow

 - Maintaining structured candidate progression

 - Preserving evaluation rigor

 - Reducing time-to-interview and time-to-offer

 - Ensuring strong candidate experience

This is an execution-critical role within the hiring pipeline.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


SOURCING & PIPELINE EXECUTION

 - Source candidates across prioritized channels

 - Maintain proactive outreach cadences

 - Build candidate pools ahead of immediate demand

 - Track sourcing effectiveness by channel


CANDIDATE SCREENING & SIGNAL FILTERING

 - Conduct structured screening interviews

 - Assess alignment against defined role rubrics

 - Filter for capability, ownership mindset, and execution ability

 - Maintain clear candidate documentation


INTERVIEW COORDINATION & PROGRESSION

 - Schedule and manage interview stages

 - Ensure timely feedback collection

 - Reduce bottlenecks in hiring workflows

 - Maintain candidate momentum through the funnel


CANDIDATE EXPERIENCE

 - Communicate clearly and professionally

 - Maintain responsiveness and transparency

 - Uphold Rwazi’s reputation in the talent market


RECRUITING METRICS & DISCIPLINE

 - Track funnel conversion rates

 - Maintain accurate pipeline reporting

 - Identify drop-off patterns and escalate issues


ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance in this role results in:

 - Faster hiring cycles

 - Higher-quality candidate shortlists

 - Reduced interview fatigue for hiring managers

 - Predictable pipeline health

 - Stronger employer brand perception




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - Not passive resume sorting

 - Not job posting only

 - Not administrative scheduling support

 - Not lowering bar to fill seats

This role requires judgment, pace, and structure.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - 2–5+ years in recruiting (startup or high-performance environments preferred)

 - Experience hiring across multiple functions (Product, Ops, Finance, etc.)

 - Strong structured interviewing ability

 - Clear written and verbal communication

 - Ability to operate with speed and organization

 - Comfort working in metric-driven environments

Candidates may come from startup recruiting, marketplace hiring, agency environments, or internal talent acquisition teams.




CULTURAL FIT

We value recruiters who:

 - Protect the hiring bar

 - Move quickly without chaos

 - Maintain high ownership

 - Operate with clarity and professionalism

 - See recruiting as a system, not a task




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Quality of sourced candidates

 - Funnel discipline and pipeline clarity

 - Communication clarity

 - Speed without compromise

 - Ability to maintain candidate momentum




SUMMARY

The Recruiter powers the execution layer of Rwazi’s hiring engine.

This role ensures high-quality talent flows predictably into the organization as we scale.

source_jobs.title: Sales Director, US
source_jobs.description:
SALES DIRECTOR, US



Team: Monetization
Location: United States
Reporting to: Head of Monetization




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi sells enterprise AI-powered decision intelligence subscriptions.

The Sales Director, US owns new revenue execution across the United States market.

This role lands new enterprise accounts and expands existing subscriptions through structured pipeline discipline, executive engagement, and value-based selling.

This is revenue ownership, not relationship management.




CORE MANDATE

The Sales Director, US is accountable for:

 - Closing new enterprise subscription revenue

 - Expanding ARR within existing accounts

 - Driving pipeline-to-close efficiency

 - Maintaining structured deal discipline

 - Representing Rwazi at executive buyer level




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own full-cycle enterprise sales (land and expand)

 - Maintain predictable forecast accuracy

 - Navigate complex buying committees

 - Align product capabilities with enterprise pain points

 - Close multi-year subscription contracts

 - Drive expansion within multi-department enterprise accounts




ROLE IMPACT

 - Increased ARR

 - Higher win rates

 - Larger deal sizes

 - Stronger US market penetration

source_jobs.title: Sales Director, UK
source_jobs.description:
SALES DIRECTOR, UK



Team: Monetization
Location: United Kingdom
Reporting to: Head of Monetization




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi sells enterprise AI-powered decision intelligence subscriptions.

The Sales Director, UK owns new revenue execution across the United Kingdom market.

This role lands new enterprise accounts and expands existing subscriptions through structured pipeline discipline, executive engagement, and value-based selling.

This is revenue ownership, not relationship management.




CORE MANDATE

The Sales Director, UK is accountable for:

 - Closing new enterprise subscription revenue

 - Expanding ARR within existing accounts

 - Driving pipeline-to-close efficiency

 - Maintaining structured deal discipline

 - Representing Rwazi at executive buyer level




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own full-cycle enterprise sales (land and expand)

 - Maintain predictable forecast accuracy

 - Navigate complex buying committees

 - Align product capabilities with enterprise pain points

 - Close multi-year subscription contracts

 - Drive expansion within multi-department enterprise accounts




ROLE IMPACT

 - Increased ARR

 - Higher win rates

 - Larger deal sizes

 - Stronger UK market penetration

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (CEO Office)
source_jobs.description:
EXECUTIVE ASSISTANT

Team: CEO Office
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: CEO




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi operates in a high-velocity, AI-native environment where clarity, follow-through, and structured thinking are essential.

The Executive Assistant supports the CEO in day-to-day executive operations — documentation, planning cadence, structured follow-ups, and internal coordination.

This is a foundational role inside the CEO’s office.

While the title is traditional, the expectation is not.

We are looking for someone with former founder instincts or strong analytical pedigree — who understands strategy, but is comfortable operating in a support capacity without ego.

This role requires intelligence, discipline, and humility.




CORE MANDATE

The Executive Assistant is accountable for:

 - Maintaining executive documentation rigor

 - Structuring meetings into clear outputs and actions

 - Tracking cross-functional commitments

 - Supporting board and planning cycles

 - Reducing cognitive load on the CEO

This role strengthens executive execution hygiene.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


EXECUTIVE DOCUMENTATION & STRUCTURING

 - Capture high-quality, structured notes in executive and board meetings

 - Translate discussions into clear action items with owners and deadlines

 - Maintain organized, searchable documentation systems

 - Draft memos, summaries, and internal briefs


PLANNING & CADENCE MANAGEMENT

 - Support quarterly and annual planning processes

 - Assist in preparing board decks and executive materials

 - Maintain visibility over key company-wide priorities

 - Ensure follow-through across functional leaders


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COORDINATION

 - Track execution commitments across teams

 - Follow up on deadlines and deliverables

 - Escalate slippage early and objectively

 - Maintain structured dashboards of ongoing initiatives


OPERATIONAL & ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT

 - Manage executive scheduling and prioritization

 - Coordinate internal and external communications

 - Maintain high professional standards in all outputs




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance results in:

 - Clear executive decision trails

 - Fewer dropped commitments

 - Tighter cross-functional alignment

 - Faster execution cycles

 - Increased CEO focus on strategic priorities




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - This is not a strategic leadership role

 - This is not independent product or revenue ownership

 - This is not a stepping stone to immediate executive authority

This role requires comfort operating in a support function — regardless of prior seniority.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - Former founder, operator, or builder experience OR strong analytical background (consulting, finance, research, technical environments)

 - Exceptional structured writing ability

 - Strong analytical reasoning and pattern recognition

 - Comfort synthesizing complex discussions into clarity

 - High attention to detail

 - Ability to operate without ego in a support capacity

 - Strong discretion and professionalism

Former founders are welcome — provided they are motivated by discipline and execution, not status.




CULTURAL FIT

We value individuals who:

 - Think sharply but act humbly

 - Prefer execution over visibility

 - Take pride in structured output

 - Care about precision

 - Follow through consistently




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Clarity and structure of written work

 - Analytical depth in summarizing complex discussions

 - Discipline in tracking commitments

 - Comfort supporting executive workflows

 - Absence of ego in operational contexts




SUMMARY

The Executive Assistant strengthens the CEO’s office by applying founder-level thinking and analytical rigor to executive documentation, planning, and follow-through.

This role is foundational to maintaining speed, clarity, and execution discipline as Rwazi scales.

source_jobs.title: Senior Community Manager
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR COMMUNITY MANAGER

Team: Community
Location: Flexible / Remote
Reporting to: VP of Community




ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi’s Community function represents the human surface area of the company — contributors, mappers, enterprise clients, and ecosystem partners.

The Senior Community Manager owns ecosystem strategy.

This role defines how these groups connect, interact, and reinforce one another within the broader Rwazi network.

Community at this level is not messaging.
It is network architecture.

The Senior Community Manager ensures Rwazi’s ecosystem compounds in trust, density, and strategic value over time.




CORE MANDATE

The Senior Community Manager is accountable for:

 - Designing the structure of Rwazi’s ecosystem

 - Strengthening relationships across users, mappers, clients, and partners

 - Creating cross-node interaction within the network

 - Increasing ecosystem resilience and loyalty

 - Protecting long-term trust capital

This role builds durable relational infrastructure.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


ECOSYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

 - Define how contributors, enterprise users, and partners interact

 - Design structured engagement models across stakeholder groups

 - Create pathways for collaboration and shared value

 - Reduce ecosystem fragmentation


MULTI-STAKEHOLDER RELATIONSHIP STRATEGY

 - Maintain strong relationships across core ecosystem segments

 - Identify trust gaps or sentiment risk early

 - Develop structured communication strategies per segment

 - Balance enterprise professionalism with grassroots authenticity


COMMUNITY-TO-PRODUCT FEEDBACK LOOPS

 - Surface structured ecosystem insights to Product and Leadership

 - Identify recurring friction patterns across stakeholders

 - Translate ecosystem signals into strategic adjustments


IDENTITY & NARRATIVE STEWARDSHIP

 - Strengthen Rwazi’s identity within its network

 - Develop rituals, recognition systems, and shared milestones

 - Protect tone, positioning, and relational clarity


STRATEGIC EXPANSION SUPPORT

 - Support VP of Community in scaling ecosystem footprint

 - Evaluate new market or segment entry from a network perspective

 - Identify ecosystem-driven growth opportunities




ROLE IMPACT

Strong performance results in:

 - Higher ecosystem cohesion

 - Increased stakeholder loyalty

 - Stronger cross-network referrals

 - Reduced churn across contributors and clients

 - Greater long-term defensibility

This role strengthens Rwazi’s human moat.




WHAT THIS ROLE IS NOT

 - Not delivery operations

 - Not reactive support

 - Not transactional messaging

 - Not community engagement for engagement’s sake

This role requires structured ecosystem thinking.




QUALIFICATIONS AND PROFILE

We are looking for individuals who demonstrate:

 - 6–10+ years in community, ecosystem, or network leadership roles

 - Experience managing multi-sided platforms (marketplace, SaaS, or distributed networks)

 - Strong relationship-building ability across different stakeholder types

 - Strategic thinking around network effects

 - Analytical comfort with retention, density, and ecosystem health metrics

 - Executive communication clarity

Candidates may come from marketplace ecosystems, enterprise SaaS community leadership, or multi-stakeholder platforms.




CULTURAL FIT

We value leaders who:

 - Think long-term about trust capital

 - Balance warmth with strategic discipline

 - Operate without ego but with authority

 - Care about ecosystem integrity

 - Prefer durable systems over reactive fixes




HOW CANDIDATES ARE EVALUATED

Candidates are evaluated based on:

 - Their ability to design and scale ecosystem structures

 - Evidence of strengthening multi-sided networks

 - Clarity in handling stakeholder complexity

 - Structured thinking around community health metrics

 - Strategic maturity in managing trust across groups




SUMMARY

The Senior Community Manager leads the design and strengthening of Rwazi’s ecosystem across users, mappers, e

source_jobs.title: Community Growth Manager

ROLE OVERVIEW

Rwazi’s Community function represents the human layer of the company — contributors, mappers, and platform participants across global markets.

USER ACQUISITION & EXPANSION

ACTIVATION VELOCITY

NETWORK DENSITY & COVERAGE

REFERRAL & ORGANIC GROWTH SYSTEMS

This role ensures the Community layer grows predictably, efficiently, and sustainably as the company scales.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
MISSION AT RWAZI

Rwazi is a decision intelligence platform powered by consumer-trained AI that helps global brands increase market share and revenue by transforming real-world consumer activity into actionable insights across 195+ countries.

Our AI identifies key opportunities and integrates zero-party consumer data from millions of users with enterprise systems for real-time, strategic guidance.

Sena, our B2B AI layer, serves as a conversational assistant and reporting engine, providing instant answers and insights from direct consumer data, which you will oversee as a designer.

---


THE ROLE

You will be the Senior Product Designer for Sena, responsible for the end-to-end experience of a data-rich, AI-native enterprise platform. You will shape the product alongside product and engineering leadership, design in a complex data context, and work closely with the code.

Product ownership. Sena is at an early and defining stage. You will influence what gets built, how intelligence is surfaced, and how enterprise clients interact with AI-generated insights. The designer here is a product thinker first.

Design-to-code workflow. Our process runs from Figma through Claude Code to production. You will use AI-assisted development tooling daily to prototype, build, and iterate on front-end implementations. This is integral to how the team operates.

Data visualization. Sena surfaces market intelligence, consumer analytics, AI-generated reports, and competitive benchmarking. You will design the AI-first, conversational experience, alongside dashboards, report layouts, and visualization systems that make dense, multi-dimensional data clear and actionable for enterprise users.

Business-oriented design. Design decisions are framed in terms of adoption, retention, time-to-value, and revenue impact. You will present and defend your work in those terms with stakeholders across the organization.

Small, senior team. You will report to the Director of Product Design and work alongside one other designer. Cross-functional collaboration with engineering and product leadership is direct and continuous.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of product design experience with substantial time spent on B2B SaaS products. Experience with enterprise dashboards, analytics platforms, reporting tools, or market intelligence products is especially relevant.

 - Fluency with AI/LLM tooling. You have integrated AI into your design or development workflow in a meaningful way. You understand how LLM-powered features behave, their constraints, and how to design for probabilistic outputs.

 - Front-end building capability. You are comfortable writing or editing HTML / CSS and have experience with AI-assisted coding tools such as Claude Code and Codex.

 - User research skills. You can conduct user interviews, extract insights, and translate findings into clear product opportunities. You can present and advocate for these opportunities with stakeholders.

 - Strong data visualization skills. You can take a dense dataset and design a clear, effective way to present it. You have thought carefully about chart types, information hierarchy, and progressive disclosure in professional contexts.

 - Comfort with ambiguity. Sena is evolving. You will sometimes need to make strong design decisions with incomplete information and iterate as you learn more.

 - Business-literate design thinking. You can articulate why a design choice matters in terms of user adoption, conversion, retention, or operational efficiency, and you have done this in front of stakeholders.


VALUED EXPERIENCE

 - Enterprise SaaS, market intelligence, research, or analytics platforms.

 - AI-native products where AI is the core value proposition.

 - Hands-on experience prototyping and building UI with Claude Code (or similar AI-assisted development tools).

 - Design systems or component library architecture.

 - Experience working across global markets and distributed teams.


HOW WE WORK

source_jobs.title: Growth Hacker (B2C)
source_jobs.description:
Rwazi pays people across the globe to complete short paid tasks — observations, logs, gigs — that turn into structured market intelligence for some of the world's largest brands. Our participants are the supply side of an exchange we sell into Fortune 500 clients. Every activated participant is capacity we monetize.

The role You own the discipline of attracting high-intent participants — not leads, not impressions, not clicks. Participants who sign up, complete paid tasks, and come back for more. You build the systematized growth engine that scales us into new geographies and deepens density in existing ones. You operate globally from Day 1.

This is not a paid-media role. We do not need someone to scale Meta or Google ad spend. We need someone who can architect channels — communities, partnerships, organic loops, referral mechanics, agentic outreach pipelines, geo-specific distribution — and prove they convert into completed paid tasks, not vanity sign-ups.

What you'll own

 - A participant acquisition engine that compounds: organic loops, referral mechanics, community partnerships, agentic outreach, geo-specific channel mapping

 - Automation and agentic workflows (LLMs for outreach personalization, scrapers for community discovery, Make/n8n/Zapier-style orchestration, lightweight SQL) — scaling acquisition without scaling headcount

 - Geographic unlocks driven by client demand and supply-side economics

 - The metrics that matter: cost per activated participant, completion rate, repeat-task rate, lifetime task count by market

 - Weekly experiments. Kill losers in two weeks. Double down on winners.

You're a fit if

 - You've grown a marketplace, gig platform, or two-sided business — and can point at the numbers you moved

 - You think in mechanisms, not campaigns ("we got 3x lift from a Telegram bot that pinged completed users with same-day tasks" beats "we ran a campaign")

 - You're fluent with agentic tooling — LLM-powered outreach, scraping, no-code orchestration, basic data work

 - You've shipped in emerging markets and know the channel mix changes by country

 - You write your own copy, your own scripts, your own dashboards — until it's time to scale, then you systematize

You're not a fit if

 - Your default move is "let me spin up Meta and Google"

 - You need a team of five to ship anything

 - You measure success in MQLs

What we offer

 - Market-rate base plus performance-tied upside on activated-participant volume

 - Full ownership of a function with direct revenue impact

 - Direct working relationship with the CEO and exec team

 - Global, remote

How to apply Send (1) your best growth experiment — what you tried, what worked, what the number was — and (2) a one-paragraph plan for how you'd attract paid task participants in three emerging markets of your choice. Generic applications get filtered. We read everything that comes with a number attached.

source_jobs.title: Problem Solver
source_jobs.description:
Before you read any further, this is probably not for you.

Most people are looking for a role. We are looking for a type of person.

That person is wired differently.

When everyone else is heading for the exit, they are walking back in. When there is no playbook, they write one overnight. When they hear "it can't be done," they hear a deadline. They do not wait for permission, budget, or instructions. They find a way, and when there is no way, they make one.

They are rare. Most companies mistake them for rebels, misfits, or troublemakers right up until they solve a problem nobody else could.

We built Rwazi around them. This page exists to find more.




WHAT RWAZI IS

Rwazi is a Decision AI company. Fortune 500 enterprises run their biggest decisions through us, and the world's most advanced AI companies come to us when they need the most complex AI datasets on earth. We are creating a category that did not exist, and the pace of that creation shapes everything about how we operate.

That speed is the point. It is also why we hire the way we do.




THE ROLE

There is no title. There is no department. There is no job description in the normal sense, because the job is the part of Rwazi that is hardest right now.

You will be handed problems that have never been solved, by us or by anyone. No precedent, no expert to call, no process to follow. Your job is to solve them anyway, and then build the system that makes your solution run without you, so you can move to the next one. Solving is the entry fee. Systematizing is the job.

The scope has no edges. What you work on this quarter may not exist as a concept today.




HOW WE WILL KNOW YOU

Nothing on your résumé will save you here. The MBA will not save you. The certifications will not save you. The decade spent doing something that looks similar will not save you, because nothing here looks like anything for long. The difference between people who thrive here and people who do not was never on paper. So we will not ask where you worked or what you studied.

What does predict success is a pattern. We have seen it often enough that we can recognize it almost immediately.

You take full ownership of outcomes. When something breaks, your first move is toward it, never away, and never toward an explanation of whose fault it was. You generate options relentlessly; when ten doors close, you have already found the eleventh. You are resourceful to the point of art, delivering more with less and never confusing motion with results. You are unreasonably resourceful. When resources are scarce, timelines are absurd, information is incomplete, and everyone else is explaining why something cannot be done, you have a habit of finding a way forward anyway.

If you read that and felt recognized, keep going. If you read it and felt tired, stop here. Both reactions are correct.

For the right person, this environment feels less like a job and more like being dropped into the middle of an expedition. Every day presents a new challenge. Every solved problem reveals a larger one behind it. Every breakthrough expands what becomes possible. Some people find that exhausting. Others spend their entire careers searching for it.




THE HONEST PART

This will be the most demanding thing you have ever done. The pace does not slow down; it compounds. The problems do not get easier; they get bigger, because every one you solve raises the ceiling. Most excellent people, people who have succeeded everywhere else, would not last here, and we say that with genuine respect for them.

We are not selling comfort. We are offering the arena: the hardest problems, total ownership, zero bureaucracy between you and the outcome, and a seat on the small team changing how business is done in the 21st century. For a specific kind of person, that trade is the best one available anywhere. You know if you are that person.




WHAT YOU GET

The mission. You are not joining a category. You are building one.

The arena. Unsol

notes: Rwazi is a product-driven AI data intelligence company with a strong focus on decision systems and AI-powered SaaS platform.
